title: Polyolefin and polycarbonate compositions with glass fiber
abstract: A thermoplastic composition comprising: 10 to 80 wt% of a polyolefin; 5 to 60 wt% of an aromatic polycarbonate; and 5 to 60 wt% of a glass fiber coated with a sizing composition, wherein a weight ratio of the polyolefin to the aromatic polycarbonate is 90:10 to 25:75, wherein the polyolefin comprises a homopolymer or copolymer comprising at least 80 wt% of units derived from polymerization of ethylene, propylene, or a combination thereof, preferably wherein the polyolefin has a heat of fusion of 20 to 120 J/g, measured by differential scanning calorimetry, and preferably wherein the thermoplastic composition has a heat deflection temperature of at least 125Â°C in accordance with ASTM D648; and a flexural modulus of greater than 400 MPa, in accordance with ASTM D790.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A thermoplastic composition comprising: 10 to 80 weight percent, preferably 25 to 75 weight percent, more preferably 30 to 70 weight percent of a polyolefin; 5 to 60 weight percent, preferably 10 to 50 weight percent, more preferably 15 to 30 weight percent of an aromatic polycarbonate; and 5 to 60 weight percent, preferably 10 to 50 weight percent, more preferably 15 to 40 weight percent of a glass fiber coated with a sizing composition, wherein all weight percent values are based on the total weight of the composition, and wherein a weight ratio of the polyolefin to the aromatic polycarbonate is 90: 10 to 25:75, preferably 85: 15 to 30:70, more preferably 70:30 to 50:50, wherein the polyolefin comprises a homopolymer or copolymer comprising at least 80 weight percent of units derived from polymerization of ethylene, propylene, or a combination thereof, preferably wherein the polyolefin has a heat of fusion of 20 to 120 J/g, measured by differential scanning calorimetry, and preferably wherein the thermoplastic composition has a heat deflection temperature of at least l25Â°C, preferably 125 to l60Â°C, more preferably 130 to l55Â°C, measured in accordance with ASTM D648 at 0.46 MPa; and a flexural modulus of greater than 400 MPa, preferably greater than 500 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l30Â°C. 2. The thermoplastic composition of claim 1, wherein the aromatic polycarbonate comprises a polycarbonate homopolymer or a polycarbonate copolymer comprising repeating units of the formula wherein R a and R b are each independently a C 1-12 alkyl group, C 1-12 alkenyl, C3-8 cycloalkyl, or C 1-12 alkoxy, p and q are each independently integers of 0 to 4, and X a is a single bond, -O-, -S-, -S(O)-, -S(0) 2 -, -C(O)-, a Ci-11 alkylidene of formula -C(R c )(R d ) - wherein R c and R d are each independently hydrogen or C1-10 alkyl, or a group of the formula -C(=R e )- wherein R e is a divalent C1-10 hydrocarbon group; 

 preferably wherein the polycarbonate comprises bisphenol A carbonate units; more preferably wherein the polycarbonate has a weight average molecular weight of 15,000 to 26,000 g/mol as determined by gel permeation chromatography using bisphenol A homopolycarbonate standards. 3. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 2, wherein the sizing composition comprises a polyepoxide; a poly(meth)acrylate; a poly(arylene ether); a polyurethane; a polyolefin; or a combination thereof. 4. The thermoplastic composition of claim 3, wherein the sizing composition further comprises a silane coupling agent, wherein the silane coupling agent comprises tri(Ci- 6 alkoxy)monoamino silane, tri(Ci- 6 alkoxy)diamino silane, tri(Ci- 6 alkoxy)(Ci- 6 alkyl ureido) silane, tri(Ci- 6 alkoxy)(epoxy Ci -6 alkyl) silane, tri(Ci- 6 alkoxy)(glycidoxy Ci -6 alkyl) silane, tri(Ci- 6 alkoxy)(mercapto Ci -6 alkyl) silane, or a combination thereof; more preferably wherein the silane coupling agent comprises (3- aminopropyl)triethoxysilane, (3-glycidoxypropyl)trimethoxysilane, (2-(3,4- epoxycyclohexyl)ethyl)triethoxysilane, (3-mercaptopropyl)trimethoxysilane, (3-(2- aminoethylamino)propyl)triethoxysilane, (3 -ureidopropyl)triethoxy silane, or a combination thereof. 5. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 4, further comprising a reactive impact modifier, wherein the reactive impact modifier is a polypropylene grafted with a carboxylic acid or a salt thereof, an anhydride, an ester, or a combination thereof, preferably wherein the reactive impact modifier is a homopolypropylene grafted with maleic anhydride. 6. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 5, further comprising 1 to 15 weight percent of an impact modifier, preferably wherein the impact modifier comprises methyl methacrylate-butadiene- styrene, acrylonitrile -butadiene-styrene, acrylonitrile-styrene-butyl acrylate, methyl methacrylate-acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene, acrylonitrile-ethylene-propylene-diene- styrene, styrene-ethylene-butylene- styrene, a poly(ether ester), or a combination thereof. 7. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 6 wherein the glass fiber is present in an amount of 10 to 20 weight percent, based on the total weight of the composition; and wherein the composition has: a heat deflection temperature of greater than l25Â°C, measured in accordance with ASTM D648 at 0.46 MPa, 

 a flexural modulus of greater than 410 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l30Â°C, a tensile modulus of greater than or equal to 2,300 MPa, measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a tensile strength of greater than or equal to 25 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than or equal to 1,900 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C, and a flexural strength of greater than or equal to 50 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C. 8. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 6, wherein the glass fiber is present in an amount of 20 to 30 weight percent, based on the total weight of the composition; and wherein the composition has: a heat deflection temperature of greater than l30Â°C, measured in accordance with ASTM D648 at 0.46 MPa, a flexural modulus of greater than 600 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l30Â°C, a tensile modulus of greater than or equal to 2,600 MPa, measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a tensile strength of greater than or equal to 25 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than or equal to 1,900 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C, and a flexural strength of greater than or equal to 40 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C. 9. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 6, wherein the glass fiber is present in an amount of 30 to 40 weight percent, based on the total weight of the composition; and wherein the composition has: a heat deflection temperature of greater than l35Â°C, measured in accordance with ASTM D648 at 0.46 MPa, a flexural modulus of greater than 200 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l45Â°C, a tensile modulus of greater than or equal to 3,500 MPa, measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, 

 a tensile strength of greater than or equal to 25 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than or equal to 3,000 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C, and a flexural strength of greater than or equal to 45 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C. 10. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 6, wherein the polycarbonate has a weight average molecular weight of 18,000 to 25,000 g/mol as determined by gel permeation chromatography using bisphenol A homopolycarbonate standards; and wherein the composition has: a heat deflection temperature of greater than l30Â°C, measured in accordance with ASTM D648 at 0.46 MPa, a flexural modulus of greater than 1,000 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l30Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than 400 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l45Â°C, a tensile modulus of greater than or equal to 4,000 MPa, measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a tensile strength of greater than or equal to 30 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than or equal to 4,000 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C, and a flexural strength of greater than or equal to 40 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C. 11. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 6, wherein the composition further comprises 10 to 30 weight percent of a halogen-substituted polycarbonate; and wherein the composition has: a heat deflection temperature of greater than l40Â°C, measured in accordance with ASTM D648 at 0.46 MPa, a flexural modulus of greater than 1,000 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l30Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than 600 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l45Â°C, 

 a flexural modulus of greater than 400 MPa, measured in accordance with ASTM D790 at l50Â°C, a tensile modulus of greater than or equal to 4,600 MPa, measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a tensile strength of greater than or equal to 30 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D638 at 5 mm/min and at 23Â°C, a flexural modulus of greater than or equal to 4,100 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C, and a flexural strength of greater than or equal to 50 MPa, when measured according to ASTM D790 at 1.27 mm/min and at 23Â°C. 12. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 11, wherein the composition is substantially free of inorganic pigments and metal oxides, preferably wherein the composition is substantially free of titanium dioxide. 13. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 12, further comprising 0.1 to 10 weight percent, preferably 0.1 to 5 weight percent, more preferably 0.1 to 2 weight percent of talc. 14. The thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 13, wherein the polyolefin is present in an amount of 30 to 65 weight percent, the aromatic polycarbonate is present in an amount of 15 to 30 weight percent, and the glass fiber coated with the sizing composition is present in an amount of 10 to 45 weight percent, and wherein the molar ratio of the polyolefin to the aromatic polycarbonate is 85:15 to 60:40; preferably further comprising 0.1 to 0.5 weight percent of talc; more preferably further comprising 1 to 8 weight percent of a homopolypropylene grafted with maleic anhydride. 15. An article having improved opacity prepared from the thermoplastic composition of any one or more of claims 1 to 14, wherein the article has a light transmission of less than 40%, preferably less than 30%, more preferably less than 20% when measured according to ASTM D1003 at a thickness of 3.2 millimeters.
